div.head: UR–7. Editorial Note
div.p:
On October 20, 1958, two contracts of sale were signed transferring all of the assets and liabilities of the two U.S. meatpacking plants, Swift and Armour, to a workers’ cooperative known as Establecimientos
Frigorificos del Cerro S.A., Uruguayan Army troops, who had been guarding the plants, were withdrawn on October 27, 1958.
